body:
## Implementation Plan Complete — BIN-77 Command Palette

Implementation plan written for universal command palette (`Cmd/Ctrl+K`).

### Summary

- **4 new files**: `CommandItem` types, `commands` lib (build + fuzzy search), `CommandPalette` component + CSS
- **3 modified files**: `AppShell` (state, global listener, mount), `Header` (trigger button), `Header.module.css`
- Global keyboard listener with toggle behavior; Escape handled in palette
- `buildCommands()` scans all stories + scenes from localStorage at open time — O(n), <5ms for 200+ scenes
- Worldbuilding group renders gracefully with empty-state when no data
- Focus restoration via `lastFocusRef` tracked before open, restored on close
- Mobile: full-screen panel, trigger button hidden, keyboard shortcut still works

See `<plan>` block in issue description for exact code, CSS, and acceptance mapping.

Assign to: Code Monkey

## Update
Design brief prepared for universal command palette with current UI architecture constraints and handoff to engineering.

- Scope anchored to writer flow recovery (<2s target interaction feel) and Living Writer-style clarity
- Compatible with current `AppShell`/header/nav layout and grayscale token system
- Source issue: [BIN-77](/BIN/issues/BIN-77)

## UX goal
Enable writers to jump to any story object or core action in 1-2 keystrokes without losing drafting context, so navigation feels instantaneous and cognitively light during long writing sessions.

## Information hierarchy
1. Query input (always focused on open)
2. Top result group: `Recent` (last opened scenes/items)
3. Core destination groups: `Scenes`, `Chapters`, `Worldbuilding`, `Stories`
4. Action group: `Quick actions` (create scene, rename scene, jump to corkboard/timeline, open settings)
5. Secondary metadata in each row: story/chapter context, mode badge (Editor/Corkboard), and shortcut hint when relevant

## Layout and components
- Use a centered modal panel over dimmed app content (not full-page route).
- Panel anatomy: search input row at top, scrollable result list in middle, compact helper footer at bottom (keyboard hints like arrows/enter/esc).
- Result row anatomy: left icon + label, right context text; active row gets strong contrast background and clear focus ring.
- Group headers stay subtle and sticky while scrolling within results for orientation in long lists.
- Keep row height compact and consistent to reduce pointer travel and support rapid arrow-key scanning.

## Interaction states
- Idle: shows `Recent` and top quick actions before typing.
- Loading: lightweight skeleton rows inside existing panel (no layout shift).
- Empty: explicit "No matches" with one recovery hint (try scene title/chapter/character name) and optional create action when valid.
- Error: inline non-blocking message at list top; keep query text so retry is instant.
- Success: on selection, close palette immediately and route/update mode; show a short confirmation toast only for mutating quick actions (create/rename), not for plain navigation.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: `Cmd/Ctrl+K` open, `Esc` close, arrows navigate, `Enter` select, `Cmd/Ctrl+1..n` optional quick group jumps only if conflict-free.
- Focus: trap focus inside palette while open; return focus to invoking element/editor caret on close.
- Contrast: use approved grayscale tokens at AA minimum for text, focus, selected-row states.
- Text clarity: plain-language labels ("Jump to Scene", "Open Corkboard") and concise metadata; avoid jargon.

## Visual style
- Match existing grayscale system from `src/styles/tokens.css`; no new accent color required for v1.
- Suggested mapping: panel/background `--color-white`, overlay tint via black alpha, borders `--color-fashion-300`, secondary text `--color-cement-400`/`--color-elephant-500`, primary text `--color-black-700`, focus/active emphasis `--color-carbon-600`, hover surfaces `--color-fog-200`/`--color-polar-100`.
- Preserve calm, low-noise, editorial feel consistent with existing header/navigation surfaces.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop/tablet: centered modal with constrained max width; results scroll internally.
- Mobile: full-width sheet style from top with safe-area padding, large tap targets, and persistent close affordance; keyboard-first still supported with external keyboard.
- Keep open/close animation subtle and fast (<200ms perceived) so it feels utility-like, not decorative.

## Codebase alignment
- Reviewed app shell structure and injection points: `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`, `src/components/Header/Header.tsx`, `src/components/Navigation/Navigation.tsx`, `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.tsx`.
- Reviewed editor/corkboard contexts to preserve mode on jump: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`, `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx`.
- Reviewed styling primitives/tokens: `src/styles/tokens.css` and existing module CSS patterns.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: token palette, spacing, typography, border radius, focus styling conventions, and existing modal z-layer semantics (`--z-modal`).
- Reuse: header action area as the visible trigger location for discoverability while keyboard shortcut remains primary.
- New build: dedicated `CommandPalette` composite (overlay + input + grouped results + footer hints) and command index adapter for scenes/chapters/worldbuilding/actions.

## Feasibility notes
- Current data layer is localStorage/mock-centric (`src/lib/scenes.ts`), so ranking freshness and recents should be scoped to available client data in v1.
- "Preserve view mode" requires passing current mode context through navigation actions (editor vs corkboard), especially from `story/[id]` route transitions.
- Worldbuilding entities are not yet represented in current route/data structure; UI should reserve group space and degrade gracefully when data source is absent.
- For 200+ scenes responsiveness, favor incremental filtering and lightweight row rendering to avoid input lag.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor palette mount high in app tree (recommended in `AppShell`) so it is globally invokable across routes.
- Add shortcut listener with strict input-guard rules (ignore when user is composing IME text or typing into focused editable fields unless palette already open).
- Respect existing header height and content container behavior (`64px` header, `ContentContainer` padding) so overlay positioning does not break layout.
- Ensure modal layering does not conflict with mobile nav overlay in `AppShell.module.css` (`--z-modal` and overlay stacking).

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
A universal, keyboard-first command palette can be a real differentiator if we make it story-aware across manuscript + worldbuilding + planner surfaces and keep navigation nearly instant. Competitors offer navigation aids, but not a clearly unified cross-surface action hub optimized for long-form drafting flow.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter documents tab-based chapter navigation ("Tabbed Viewing") and positions it as manuscript navigation support, but this is chapter-tab oriented rather than a universal action/search launcher: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/tabbed-viewing
- LivingWriter’s September 1, 2024 changelog highlights "Tabbed Navigation" as a major improvement, reinforcing that their navigation model is tab-centric: https://guides.livingwriter.com/whats-new/changelog/september-1st-2024
- Campfire emphasizes tagged manuscript elements and sidebar note access (strong context linking), but does not present a global command palette interaction pattern on its core product page: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- In adjacent writing-productivity UX, command palettes are established expectation for power users (Notion Mail: Cmd/Ctrl+K opens command palette), so matching baseline is expected; differentiation comes from domain-specific execution quality: https://www.notion.com/help/notion-mail-keyboard-shortcuts

## Risks / trade-offs
- If fuzzy ranking quality is weak, this becomes noisy and harms trust.
- Too many quick actions in v1 can overwhelm users and increase accidental destructive actions.
- Performance regression on large manuscripts (200+ scenes) would negate the value proposition.

## Recommendation
Approve with a differentiation guardrail: Design should frame this as "flow recovery in <2 seconds" and prioritize a calm, low-cognitive UI that supports rapid repeat use. Prioritize result grouping (Recent, Scenes, Chapters, Entities, Actions), strong keyboard affordances, and clear destructive-action confirmation patterns.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Review Request
Requesting differentiation and scope validation before engineering planning.

- Focus: keyboard-first speed advantage for complex manuscripts
- Ask: approve/reframe with competitor-backed thresholds for search depth and performance

Assign to: Market Research
